attributes.description: Alerts when `envoy.server.days_until_first_cert_expiring` falls below 7 days. Expired TLS certificates cause handshake failures on downstream and upstream connections.
attributes.name: [Envoy OTel] Certificate expiring soon

investigation_guide.blob:
## Envoy certificate expiring soon

### What fired
`envoy.server.days_until_first_cert_expiring` dropped below 7 days in the evaluation window.

### Why it matters
Envoy serves TLS on the downstream and may also present client certs upstream. An expired cert breaks every secured connection, causing immediate availability impact.

### Triage
1. Identify which certificate(s) are expiring on the affected Envoy instance via the admin interface `/certs` endpoint.
2. Check the certificate issuance pipeline (cert-manager, Vault, ACME client) for stuck renewals.
3. Verify downstream clients can still connect if renewal is already in flight.

### Remediation
- Force-renew the certificate via the issuance pipeline.
- Push a new Envoy secret via SDS / hot-restart if not auto-refreshed.
- Adopt automated rotation if this is a recurring event.

### Tuning
- 7 days is a late threshold. For production, pair this rule with a 30-day warning rule.

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-statsdreceiver-default
| WHERE data_stream.dataset == "statsdreceiver"
| WHERE envoy.server.days_until_first_cert_expiring IS NOT NULL
// Days until first TLS cert expires — alert when below threshold
| STATS min_days = MIN(envoy.server.days_until_first_cert_expiring)
// Adjust threshold (7 days) based on your cert renewal lead time
| WHERE min_days < 7
